content: On June 14, 1973, the Atlanta Braves played a thrilling game against the Philadelphia Phillies, highlighted by a remarkable performance from pitcher Mike McQueen. McQueen threw a 10-inning complete game, allowing only two runs and leading the Braves to a 3-2 victory. This game was notable not only for McQueen's endurance on the mound but also for showcasing the Braves' resilience during a season that would ultimately see them finish fourth in the National League West.
